rationale: Review 1's sole finding rests on two errors: it attributes claims to 'the PR description' that do not appear anywhere in the diff, and it asserts antipodeComp 'does not depend on f' when it manifestly does (it computes f ∘ S, requiring f). Acting on Review 1 would waste effort on a restructuring that doesn't work as proposed; Review 2 correctly approves the PR as a sound reuse of existing Mathlib infrastructure.
